f3ww8rgva3hrsupdate /* KSXM:FLUSH COL */ sys.col_usage$ set equality_preds = equality_preds + decode(bitand(:flag, 1), 0, 0, 1), equijoin_preds = equijoin_preds + decode(bitand(:flag, 2), 0, 0, 1), nonequijoin_preds = nonequijoin_preds + decode(bitand(:flag, 4), 0, 0, 1), range_preds = range_preds + decode(bitand(:flag, 8), 0, 0, 1), like_preds = like_preds + decode(bitand(:flag, 16), 0, 0, 1), null_preds = null_preds + decode(bitand(:flag, 32), 0, 0, 1), flags = flags + :flag - bitand(flags, :flag), timestamp = :time where obj# = :objn and intcol# = :coln
f5ptm76xmxqrxSELECT INTERNAL_KEY, CLIENT_NO, CLIENT_TYPE, INDIVIDUAL_FLAG, DOCUMENT_ID, DOCUMENT_TYPE, ISS_COUNTRY, ACCT_CLASS, CARD_NO, BASE_ACCT_NO, PROD_TYPE, ACCT_CCY, ACCT_SEQ_NO, PROFIT_CENTER, REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(ACCT_NAME, chr(9), ''), chr(10), ''), chr(13), '') AS ACCT_NAME, ACCT_NAME_PREFIX, ACCT_NAME_SUFFIX, ALT_ACCT_NAME, ACCT_BRANCH, ACCT_DESC, SOURCE_TYPE, ACCT_OPEN_DATE, EFFECT_DATE, OPEN_TRAN_DATE, (CASE WHEN ACCT_CLOSE_DATE IS NOT NULL AND ACCT_CLOSE_DATE > to_date('20260602', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AND ACCT_STATUS='C' THEN ACCT_STATUS_PREV ELSE ACCT_STATUS END ) AS ACCT_STATUS, ACCT_STATUS_PREV, ACCT_STATUS_UPD_DATE, (CASE WHEN ACCT_CLOSE_DATE IS NOT NULL AND ACCT_CLOSE_DATE > to_date('20260602', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AND ACCT_STATUS='C' THEN NULL ELSE ACCT_CLOSE_DATE END ) AS ACCT_CLOSE_DATE, ACCT_CLOSE_USER_ID, ACCT_CLOSE_REASON, ACCT_TYPE, TERM, TERM_TYPE, MATURITY_DATE, ORI_MATURITY_DATE, ORIG_ACCT_OPEN_DATE, ACCT_NATURE, ACCT_DUE_DATE, ACCT_REAL_FLAG, ACCT_RES_STATUS, ACCT_STOP_PAY, ALL_DEP_IND, ALL_DRA_IND, FIXED_CALL, LEAD_ACCT_FLAG, ACCT_LICENSE_DATE, ACCT_LICENSE_NO, MAIN_PROD_TYPE, MAIN_BAL_FLAG, MAIN_INT_FLAG, PARENT_INTERNAL_KEY, REASON_CODE, ACCOUNTING_STATUS_UPD_DATE, APPROVAL_DATE, AUTO_RENEW_ROLLOVER, PARTIAL_RENEW_ROLL, TIMES_RENEWED, RENEW_NO, ROLLOVER_NO, TIMES_ROLLEDOVER, LAST_MVMT_STATUS, ADDTL_PRINCIPAL, DORMANT_DATE, LAST_TRAN_DATE, SETTLE_DATE, APPLY_BRANCH, HOME_BRANCH, DOC_TYPE, PREFIX, VOUCHER_START_NO, VOUCHER_STATUS, APPR_LETTER_NO, INT_IND_FLAG, XRATE, APPR_FLAG, AUTO_SETTLE_FLAG, BAL_TYPE, GL_TYPE, MULTI_BAL_TYPE_FLAG, NOTICE_PERIOD, NO_TRAN_FLAG, OSA_FLAG, OWNERSHIP_TYPE, REGION_FLAG, ACCOUNTING_STATUS, ACCOUNTING_STATUS_PREV, ACCT_EXEC, CHECKED_FLAG, MANAGEMENT_FREE_FLAG, XRATE_ID, OLD_PROD_TYPE, IMPOUND_FAD, BUSINESS_UNIT, CUR_STAGE_NO, SETTLE, SETTLE_USER_ID, MM_REF_NO, DAC_VALUE, SOURCE_MODULE, TERMINAL_ID, APPR_USER_ID, USER_ID, LAST_CHANGE_DATE, LAST_CHANGE_USER_ID, TRAN_TIMESTAMP, COMPANY, RESERVE_FLAG FROM RB_ACCT WHERE LAST_CHANGE_DATE >=to_date('20260602', 'YYYY-MM-DD')
f6rksyrvy6y5wSELECT BASE_ACCT_NO, ACCT_CCY, ACCT_SEQ_NO, PROD_TYPE, CLIENT_NO, LIMIT_REF, LIMIT_MAX_AMT, LIMIT_MIN_AMT, TRAN_TIMESTAMP, COMPANY, MAX_NUM, CHANGE_NUM, MATURE_DATE, TEMP_LIMIT_AMT, TEMP_LIMIT_NUM FROM RB_LM_CLIENT_TRAN_LIMIT WHERE SUBSTR(REPLACE(SUBSTR(TRAN_TIMESTAMP, 0, 10), '-', ''), 0, 8) >='20260602'
f7xnvp44nukp7insert into wrm$_snapshot_details (snap_id, dbid, instance_number, table_id, begin_time, end_time, step_id) values (:snap_id, :dbid, :instance_number, :bind1, :bind2, :bind3, :step_id)
fhf8upax5cxszBEGIN sys.dbms_auto_report_internal.i_save_report (:rep_ref, :snap_id, :pr_class, :rep_id, :suc); END;
ft0n2fyjcgappSELECT a.con_id as con_id, NVL (t.name, 'CDB$ROOT') AS DB_NAME, a.tablespace_name as TABLESPACE_NAME, a.bytes_alloc ALLOC_SIZE, NVL(b.bytes_free, 0) FREE_SIZE, (a.bytes_alloc - NVL (b.bytes_free, 0)) USED_SIZE, ROUND ((NVL (b.bytes_free, 0) / a.bytes_alloc) * 100, 2) Pct_Free, 100 - ROUND ((NVL (b.bytes_free, 0) / a.bytes_alloc) * 100, 2) Pct_used, maxbytes as MAX_SIZE, c.status, c.contents FROM ( SELECT f.con_id, f.tablespace_name, SUM (f.bytes) bytes_alloc, SUM ( DECODE (f.autoextensible, 'YES', f.maxbytes, 'NO', f.bytes)) maxbytes FROM cdb_data_files f GROUP BY con_id, tablespace_name) a, ( SELECT con_id, tablespace_name, SUM (bytes) bytes_free FROM cdb_free_space GROUP BY con_id, tablespace_name) b, cdb_tablespaces c, v$pdbs t WHERE a.tablespace_name = b.tablespace_name(+) AND a.con_id = b.con_id(+) AND a.tablespace_name = c.tablespace_name AND a.con_id = c.con_id AND a.con_id = t.con_id(+) UNION ALL SELECT h.con_id, NVL (t.name, 'CDB$ROOT') AS db_name, h.tablespace_name, SUM (h.bytes_free + h.bytes_used) megs_alloc, SUM ((h.bytes_free + h.bytes_used) - NVL (p.bytes_used, 0)) megs_free, SUM (NVL (p.bytes_used, 0)) megs_used, ROUND ( ( SUM ((h.bytes_free + h.bytes_used) - NVL (p.bytes_used, 0)) / SUM (h.bytes_used + h.bytes_free)) * 100, 2) Pct_Free, 100 - ROUND ( ( SUM ((h.bytes_free + h.bytes_used) - NVL (p.bytes_used, 0)) / SUM (h.bytes_used + h.bytes_free)) * 100, 2) pct_used, SUM (DECODE (f.autoextensible, 'YES', f.maxbytes, 'NO', f.bytes) ) MAX, c.status, c.contents FROM (SELECT DISTINCT * FROM CONTAINERS ("SYS"."V_$TEMP_SPACE_HEADER")) h, (SELECT DISTINCT * FROM CONTAINERS ("SYS"."V_$TEMP_EXTENT_POOL")) p, cdb_temp_files f, cdb_tablespaces c, v$pdbs t WHERE p.file_id(+) = h.file_id AND p.tablespace_name(+) = h.tablespace_name AND f.file_id = h.file_id AND f.tablespace_name = h.tablespace_name AND p.con_id(+) = h.con_id AND f.con_id = h.con_id AND f.tablespace_name = c.tablespace_name AND f.con_id = c.con_id AND f.con_id = t.con_id(+) GROUP BY h.con_id, t.name, h.tablespace_name, c.status, c.contents ORDER BY 1, 2, 3
fu32utrqa7k62select col.column_name, cons.constraint_type from all_cons_columns col, all_constraints cons where col.owner = 'ENS_RB001' and col.table_name = 'RB_RESTRAINTS' and col.table_name = cons.table_name and col.constraint_name = cons.constraint_name and col.owner = cons.owner and cons.constraint_type in ('P') order by col.column_name
fxxgc62md5f59select col.column_name, cons.constraint_type from all_cons_columns col, all_constraints cons where col.owner = 'ENS_CIF002' and col.table_name = 'CIF_CLIENT' and col.table_name = cons.table_name and col.constraint_name = cons.constraint_name and col.owner = cons.owner and cons.constraint_type in ('P') order by col.column_name
g1uadbytcb6t9SELECT INTERNAL_KEY, BASE_ACCT_NO, REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(ACCT_NAME, chr(9), ''), chr(10), ''), chr(13), '') AS ACCT_NAME, ACCT_BRANCH, OTH_BASE_ACCT_NO, REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(OTH_ACCT_NAME, chr(9), ''), chr(10), ''), chr(13), '') AS OTH_ACCT_NAME, OTH_BRANCH, TRAN_TYPE, TRAN_AMT, TRAN_DATE, TRAN_BRANCH, COMPANY, REFERENCE, USER_ID, TRAN_TIMESTAMP, CLIENT_NO, CASH_ITEM, CHANNEL_SEQ_NO, SUB_SEQ_NO, CASH_SOURCE_GO, SEQ_NO FROM RB_DEPOSIT_DRAW_REG WHERE TRAN_DATE=to_date('20260602', 'YYYY-MM-DD')
gd28w82ct6rvaselect audit$ from tab$ where obj# = :1
gnutm6vw2wfh6SELECT GL_SEQ_NO, CHANNEL_SEQ_NO, INTERNAL_KEY, PROD_TYPE, BASE_ACCT_NO, ACCT_CCY, ACCT_SEQ_NO, ACCT_BRANCH, CLIENT_NO, CLIENT_TYPE, CHANNEL_DATE, EFFECT_DATE, TRAN_DATE, TRAN_BRANCH, TRAN_TYPE, CR_DR_IND, EVENT_TYPE, CCY, GL_CODE, MARKETING_PROD, MARKETING_PROD_DESC, FLAT_RATE, SUB_SEQ_NO, REFERENCE, AMOUNT, AMT_TYPE, INT_AMT, ODI_AMT, PRI_AMT, TAX_AMT, CONTRA_EQUIV_AMT, ODP_AMT, CROSS_RATE, SPREAD_PERCENT, GL_POSTED_FLAG, IN_STATUS, REVERSAL_FLAG, ACCOUNTING_STATUS, BANK_SEQ_NO, BUSINESS_UNIT, TRAN_CATEGORY, TRAN_PROFIT_CENTER, PROFIT_CENTER, SEND_SYSTEM, SOURCE_TYPE, SOURCE_MODULE, SYSTEM_ID, UN_REAL, RESERVE1, REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(NARRATIVE, chr(9), ''), chr(10), ''), chr(13), '') AS NARRATIVE, TRAN_TIMESTAMP, COMPANY, USER_ID, REVERSAL_DATE, REVERSAL_SEQ_NO, REVERSAL, TAE_SUB_SEQ_NO, INTER_TRAN_TYPE, BALANCE_CHANGE_TYPE, DEAL_FLAG, RULE_NO, SOURCE_FILE FROM RB_GL_HIST WHERE TRAN_DATE >=to_date('20260602', 'YYYY-MM-DD')
gnz4vf5g2wg4pSELECT PROD_TYPE, CHANNEL, RES_CLASS, TRAN_TYPE, EVENT_TYPE , RESTRAINT_TYPE, TRAN_TIMESTAMP, EXPRESSION, SERV_ID, COMPANY FROM RB_RESTRAINT_CONTROL_DETAILS WHERE (PROD_TYPE = :1 OR PROD_TYPE = 'ALL') AND (TRAN_TYPE = :2 OR TRAN_TYPE = 'ALL') AND (EVENT_TYPE = :3 OR EVENT_TYPE = 'ALL') AND (RES_CLASS = :4 OR RES_CLASS = 'ALL') AND (RESTRAINT_TYPE = :5 OR RESTRAINT_TYPE = 'ALL') AND EXPRESSION = :6 AND SERV_ID = :7

PGA Aggr Target Stats

  • B: Begin Snap E: End Snap (rows dentified with B or E contain data which is absolute i.e. not diffed over the interval)
  • Auto PGA Target - actual workarea memory target
  • W/A PGA Used - amount of memory used for all Workareas (manual + auto)
  • %PGA W/A Mem - percentage of PGA memory allocated to workareas
  • %Auto W/A Mem - percentage of workarea memory controlled by Auto Mem Mgmt
  • %Man W/A Mem - percentage of workarea memory under manual control

Shared Pool Advisory

  • SP: Shared Pool Est LC: Estimated Library Cache Factr: Factor
  • Note there is often a 1:Many correlation between a single logical object in the Library Cache, and the physical number of memory objects associated with it. Therefore comparing the number of Lib Cache objects (e.g. in v$librarycache), with the number of Lib Cache Memory Objects is invalid.

Undo Segment Summary

  • Min/Max TR (mins) - Min and Max Tuned Retention (minutes)
  • STO - Snapshot Too Old count, OOS - Out of Space count
  • Undo segment block stats:
  • uS - unexpired Stolen, uR - unexpired Released, uU - unexpired reUsed
  • eS - expired Stolen, eR - expired Released, eU - expired reUsed

Top SQL with Top Events

  • Top SQL statements by DB Time along with the top events by DB Time for those SQLs.
  • % Activity is the percentage of DB Time due to the SQL.
  • % Event is the percentage of DB Time due to the event that the SQL is waiting on.
  • % Row Source is the percentage of DB Time due to the row source for the SQL waiting on the event.
  • Executions is the number of executions of the SQL that were sampled in ASH.

Top SQL with Top Row Sources

  • Top SQL statements by DB Time along with the top row sources by DB Time for those SQLs.
  • % Activity is the percentage of DB Time due to the SQL.
  • % Row Source is the percentage of DB Time spent on the row source by that SQL.
  • % Event is the percentage of DB Time spent on the event by the SQL executing the row source.
  • Executions is the number of executions of the SQL that were sampled in ASH.

Top Sessions

  • '# Samples Active' shows the number of ASH samples in which the session was found waiting for that particular event. The percentage shown in this column is calculated with respect to wall clock time and not total database activity.
  • 'XIDs' shows the number of distinct transaction IDs sampled in ASH when the session was waiting for that particular event

Back to Top

Top Blocking Sessions

  • Blocking session activity percentages are calculated with respect to waits on enqueues, latches and "buffer busy" only
  • '% Activity' represents the load on the database caused by a particular blocking session
  • '# Samples Active' shows the number of ASH samples in which the blocking session was found active.
  • 'XIDs' shows the number of distinct transaction IDs sampled in ASH when the blocking session was found active.
Blocking Sid (Inst)% ActivityEvent Caused% EventUserProgram# Samples ActiveXIDs
9873,20502( 1)1.01log file sync1.01 SYSoracle@pa00db01 (LGWR)9/1,083 [ 1%]0

Back to Top

Top PL/SQL Procedures

  • 'PL/SQL entry subprogram' represents the application's top-level entry-point (procedure, function, trigger, package initialization or RPC call) into PL/SQL.
  • 'PL/SQL current subprogram' is the PL/SQL subprogram being executed at the point of sampling . If the value is 'SQL', it represents the percentage of time spent executing SQL for the particular PL/SQL entry subprogram
PL/SQL Entry Subprogram% ActivityPL/SQL Current Subprogram% CurrentContainer Name

Activity Over Time

  • Analysis period is divided into smaller time slots as indicated in the 'Slot Time (Duration)'.
  • Top 3 events are reported in each of those slots
  • 'Slot Count' shows the number of ASH samples in that slot
  • 'Event Count' shows the number of ASH samples waiting for that event in that slot
  • '% Event' is 'Event Count' over all ASH samples in the analysis period

ADDM Task ADDM:687314891_1_66536

          ADDM Report for Task 'ADDM:687314891_1_66536'
          ---------------------------------------------

Analysis Period
---------------
AWR snapshot range from 66535 to 66536.
Time period starts at 03-JUN-26 12.30.27 AM
Time period ends at 03-JUN-26 01.00.32 AM

Analysis Target
---------------
Database 'HACBSDB' with DB ID 687314891.
Database version 19.0.0.0.0.
ADDM performed an analysis of instance hacbs1, numbered 1 and hosted at
pa00db01.
ADDM detected that the database type is MULTITENANT DB.

Activity During the Analysis Period
-----------------------------------
Total database time was 1064 seconds.
The average number of active sessions was .59.

Summary of Findings
-------------------
   Description                     Active Sessions      Recommendations
                                   Percent of Activity
   ------------------------------  -------------------  ---------------
1  Global Cache Messaging          .17 | 28.42          0
2  "User I/O" wait Class           .11 | 18.26          0
3  Session Connect and Disconnect  .01 | 2.26           1


~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~


          Findings and Recommendations
          ----------------------------

Finding 1: Global Cache Messaging
Impact is .17 active sessions, 28.42% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
Inter-instance messaging was consuming significant database time on this
instance.
The network latency of the cluster interconnect was within acceptable limits
of 1 milliseconds.
Read and write contention on database blocks was not consuming significant
database time in the cluster.
Global Cache Service Processes (LMSn) in other instances were performing
within acceptable limits of 1 milliseconds.
Waits on "buffer busy" events were not consuming significant database time.

   No recommendations are available.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "Cluster" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is .17 active sessions, 29.46% of total activity.


Finding 2: "User I/O" wait Class
Impact is .11 active sessions, 18.26% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
Wait class "User I/O" was consuming significant database time.
Waits for I/O to temporary tablespaces were not consuming significant database
time.
The throughput of the I/O subsystem was not significantly lower than expected.

   No recommendations are available.


Finding 3: Session Connect and Disconnect
Impact is .01 active sessions, 2.26% of total activity.
-------------------------------------------------------
Session connect and disconnect calls were consuming significant database time.

   Recommendation 1: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is .01 active sessions, 2.26% of total activity.
   ------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate application logic for possible reduction of connect and
      disconnect calls. For example, you might use a connection pool scheme in
      the middle tier.



~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

          Additional Information
          ----------------------

Miscellaneous Information
-------------------------
Wait class "Application" was not consuming significant database time.
Wait class "Commit" was not consuming significant database time.
Wait class "Concurrency" was not consuming significant database time.
Wait class "Configuration" was not consuming significant database time.
CPU was not a bottleneck for the instance.
Wait class "Network" was not consuming significant database time.
The network latency of the cluster interconnect was within acceptable limits
of 1 milliseconds.
Hard parsing of SQL statements was not consuming significant database time.

ADDM Task ADDM:687314891_1_66539

          ADDM Report for Task 'ADDM:687314891_1_66539'
          ---------------------------------------------

Analysis Period
---------------
AWR snapshot range from 66538 to 66539.
Time period starts at 03-JUN-26 02.00.43 AM
Time period ends at 03-JUN-26 02.30.49 AM

Analysis Target
---------------
Database 'HACBSDB' with DB ID 687314891.
Database version 19.0.0.0.0.
ADDM performed an analysis of instance hacbs1, numbered 1 and hosted at
pa00db01.
ADDM detected that the database type is MULTITENANT DB.

Activity During the Analysis Period
-----------------------------------
Total database time was 854 seconds.
The average number of active sessions was .47.

Summary of Findings
-------------------
   Description                         Active Sessions      Recommendations
                                       Percent of Activity
   ----------------------------------  -------------------  ---------------
1  Top SQL Statements                  .15 | 32.1           2
2  "User I/O" wait Class               .12 | 25.18          0
3  Hard Parse Due to Sharing Criteria  .02 | 4.27           1
4  Global Cache Messaging              .01 | 2.73           0


~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~


          Findings and Recommendations
          ----------------------------

Finding 1: Top SQL Statements
Impact is .15 active sessions, 32.1% of total activity.
-------------------------------------------------------
SQL statements consuming significant database time were found. These
statements offer a good opportunity for performance improvement.

   Recommendation 1: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is .11 active sessions, 23.46% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Run SQL Tuning Advisor on the SELECT statement with SQL_ID
      "as2dr3ag24gay".
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID as2dr3ag24gay.
         select not_stale.obj# from (select s.obj# obj#, count(*) cnt,
         max(timestamp) max_time from optstat_snapshot$ s,
         wri$_optstat_tab_history t where s.obj# = t.obj# and bitand(s.flags,
         :gather_stats_flag) > 0 and t.analyzetime = (select max(analyzetime)
         from wri$_optstat_tab_history where analyzetime < s.timestamp) and
         dbms_stats_internal.is_stale(s.obj#, null, null, s.inserts +
         s.updates + s.deletes, t.rowcnt, 0) is null group by s.obj#)
         not_stale, (select obj# obj#, count(*) cnt from optstat_snapshot$
         where bitand(flags, :gather_stats_flag) > 0 group by obj#) total,
         (select obj#, max(timestamp) max_time from optstat_snapshot$ group by
         obj#) max where not_stale.obj# = total.obj# and not_stale.cnt > 0.5 *
         total.cnt and not_stale.obj# = max.obj# and not_stale.max_time =
         max.max_time and dbms_stats_internal.check_advisor_obj_filter(:rule_i
         d, not_stale.obj#, :exec_mode) = 'T'
   Rationale
      The SQL statement executed in the root container.
   Rationale
      The SQL spent 100% of its database time on CPU, I/O and Cluster waits.

Finding 2: "User I/O" wait Class
Impact is .12 active sessions, 25.18% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
Wait class "User I/O" was consuming significant database time.
Waits for I/O to temporary tablespaces were not consuming significant database
time.
The throughput of the I/O subsystem was not significantly lower than expected.

   No recommendations are available.


Finding 3: Hard Parse Due to Sharing Criteria
Impact is .02 active sessions, 4.27% of total activity.
-------------------------------------------------------
SQL statements with the same text were not shared because of cursor
environment mismatch. This resulted in additional hard parses which were
consuming significant database time.
Common causes of environment mismatch are session NLS settings, SQL trace
settings and optimizer parameters.

   Recommendation 1: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is .02 active sessions, 4.27% of total activity.
   ------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Look for top reason for cursor environment mismatch in
      V$SQL_SHARED_CURSOR.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Hard parsing of SQL statements was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is .03 active sessions, 5.68% of total activity.


Finding 4: Global Cache Messaging
Impact is .01 active sessions, 2.73% of total activity.
-------------------------------------------------------
Inter-instance messaging was consuming significant database time on this
instance.
The network latency of the cluster interconnect was within acceptable limits
of 1 milliseconds.
Read and write contention on database blocks was not consuming significant
database time in the cluster.
Global Cache Service Processes (LMSn) in other instances were performing
within acceptable limits of 1 milliseconds.
Waits on "buffer busy" events were not consuming significant database time.

   No recommendations are available.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "Cluster" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is .02 active sessions, 4.29% of total activity.

          Findings and Recommendations
          ----------------------------

Finding 1: "User I/O" wait Class
Impact is .1 active sessions, 35.02% of total activity.
-------------------------------------------------------
Wait class "User I/O" was consuming significant database time.
Waits for I/O to temporary tablespaces were not consuming significant database
time.
The throughput of the I/O subsystem was not significantly lower than expected.

   No recommendations are available.


Finding 2: Top SQL Statements
Impact is .08 active sessions, 30% of total activity.
-----------------------------------------------------
SQL statements consuming significant database time were found. These
statements offer a good opportunity for performance improvement.

   Recommendation 1: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is .03 active sessions, 10% of total activity.
   ----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate the SELECT statement with SQL_ID "ft0n2fyjcgapp" for
      possible performance improvements. You can supplement the information
      given here with an ASH report for this SQL_ID.
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID ft0n2fyjcgapp.
         SELECT a.con_id as con_id,
         NVL (t.name, 'CDB$ROOT') AS DB_NAME,
         a.tablespace_name as TABLESPACE_NAME,
         a.bytes_alloc ALLOC_SIZE,
         NVL(b.bytes_free, 0) FREE_SIZE,
         (a.bytes_alloc - NVL (b.bytes_free, 0)) USED_SIZE,
         ROUND ((NVL (b.bytes_free, 0) / a.bytes_alloc) * 100, 2) Pct_Free,
         100 - ROUND ((NVL (b.bytes_free, 0) / a.bytes_alloc) * 100, 2)
         Pct_used,
         maxbytes as MAX_SIZE,
         c.status,
         c.contents
         FROM ( SELECT f.con_id,
         f.tablespace_name,
         SUM (f.bytes) bytes_alloc,
         SUM ( DECODE (f.autoextensible,'YES', f.maxbytes,'NO', f.bytes))
         maxbytes
         FROM cdb_data_files f
         GROUP BY con_id, tablespace_name) a,
         ( SELECT con_id, tablespace_name, SUM (bytes) bytes_free
         FROM cdb_free_space
         GROUP BY con_id, tablespace_name) b,
         cdb_tablespaces c,
         v$pdbs t
         WHERE a.tablespace_name = b.tablespace_name(+)
         AND a.con_id = b.con_id(+)
         AND a.tablespace_name = c.tablespace_name
         AND a.con_id = c.con_id
         AND a.con_id = t.con_id(+)
         UNION ALL
         SELECT h.con_id,
         NVL (t.name, 'CDB$ROOT') AS db_name,
         h.tablespace_name,
         SUM (h.bytes_free + h.bytes_used) megs_alloc,
         SUM ((h.bytes_free + h.bytes_used) - NVL (p.bytes_used, 0))
         megs_free,
         SUM (NVL (p.bytes_used, 0)) megs_used,
         ROUND ( ( SUM ((h.bytes_free + h.bytes_used) - NVL (p.bytes_used, 0))
         / SUM (h.bytes_used + h.bytes_free)) * 100,2) Pct_Free,
         100 - ROUND ( ( SUM ((h.bytes_free + h.bytes_used) - NVL
         (p.bytes_used, 0)) / SUM (h.bytes_used + h.bytes_free)) * 100,2)
         pct_used,
         SUM (DECODE (f.autoextensible, 'YES', f.maxbytes,'NO', f.bytes) )
         MAX,
         c.status,
         c.contents
         FROM (SELECT DISTINCT * FROM CONTAINERS
         ("SYS"."V_$TEMP_SPACE_HEADER")) h,
         (SELECT DISTINCT * FROM CONTAINERS ("SYS"."V_$TEMP_EXTENT_POOL")) p,
         cdb_temp_files f,
         cdb_tablespaces c,
         v$pdbs t
         WHERE p.file_id(+) = h.file_id
         AND p.tablespace_name(+) = h.tablespace_name
         AND f.file_id = h.file_id
         AND f.tablespace_name = h.tablespace_name
         AND p.con_id(+) = h.con_id
         AND f.con_id = h.con_id
         AND f.tablespace_name = c.tablespace_name
         AND f.con_id = c.con_id
         AND f.con_id = t.con_id(+)
         GROUP BY h.con_id, t.name, h.tablespace_name, c.status, c.contents
         ORDER BY 1, 2, 3
   Rationale
      The SQL statement executed in container CBSH03DB with database ID
      105618639.
   Rationale
      The SQL spent only 0% of its database time on CPU, I/O and Cluster
      waits. Therefore, the SQL Tuning Advisor is not applicable in this case.
      Look at performance data for the SQL to find potential improvements.
   Rationale
      Database time for this SQL was divided as follows: 100% for SQL
      execution, 0% for parsing, 0% for PL/SQL execution and 0% for Java
      execution.
   Rationale
      Waiting for event "control file sequential read" in wait class "System
      I/O" accounted for 100% of the database time spent in processing the SQL
      statement with SQL_ID "ft0n2fyjcgapp".

   Recommendation 2: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is .03 active sessions, 10% of total activity.
   ----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate the SELECT statement with SQL_ID "ft0n2fyjcgapp" for
      possible performance improvements. You can supplement the information
      given here with an ASH report for this SQL_ID.

         SUM (NVL (p.bytes_used, 0)) megs_used,
         ROUND ( ( SUM ((h.bytes_free + h.bytes_used) - NVL (p.bytes_used, 0))
         / SUM (h.bytes_used + h.bytes_free)) * 100,2) Pct_Free,
         100 - ROUND ( ( SUM ((h.bytes_free + h.bytes_used) - NVL
         (p.bytes_used, 0)) / SUM (h.bytes_used + h.bytes_free)) * 100,2)
         pct_used,
         SUM (DECODE (f.autoextensible, 'YES', f.maxbytes,'NO', f.bytes) )
         MAX,
         c.status,
         c.contents
         FROM (SELECT DISTINCT * FROM CONTAINERS
         ("SYS"."V_$TEMP_SPACE_HEADER")) h,
         (SELECT DISTINCT * FROM CONTAINERS ("SYS"."V_$TEMP_EXTENT_POOL")) p,
         cdb_temp_files f,
         cdb_tablespaces c,
         v$pdbs t
         WHERE p.file_id(+) = h.file_id
         AND p.tablespace_name(+) = h.tablespace_name
         AND f.file_id = h.file_id
         AND f.tablespace_name = h.tablespace_name
         AND p.con_id(+) = h.con_id
         AND f.con_id = h.con_id
         AND f.tablespace_name = c.tablespace_name
         AND f.con_id = c.con_id
         AND f.con_id = t.con_id(+)
         GROUP BY h.con_id, t.name, h.tablespace_name, c.status, c.contents
         ORDER BY 1, 2, 3

Finding 3: Hard Parse Due to Sharing Criteria
Impact is .01 active sessions, 2.8% of total activity.
------------------------------------------------------
SQL statements with the same text were not shared because of cursor
environment mismatch. This resulted in additional hard parses which were
consuming significant database time.
Common causes of environment mismatch are session NLS settings, SQL trace
settings and optimizer parameters.

   Recommendation 1: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is .01 active sessions, 2.8% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Look for top reason for cursor environment mismatch in
      V$SQL_SHARED_CURSOR.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Hard parsing of SQL statements was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is .01 active sessions, 4.19% of total activity.


Finding 4: Unusual "Cluster" Wait Event
Impact is .01 active sessions, 2.19% of total activity.
-------------------------------------------------------
Wait event "gc cr disk read" in wait class "Cluster" was consuming significant
database time.

   Recommendation 1: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is .01 active sessions, 2.19% of total activity.
   ------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate the cause for high "gc cr disk read" waits. Refer to
      Oracle's "Database Reference" for the description of this wait event.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "Cluster" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is .01 active sessions, 3.61% of total activity.

Global Cache Transfer Statistics

  • Immediate (Immed) - Block Transfer NOT impacted by Remote Processing Delays
  • Busy (Busy) - Block Transfer impacted by Remote Contention
  • Congested (Congst) - Block Transfer impacted by Remote System Load

Interconnect Statistics

  • Interconnect Latency Stats

Back to Top

Interconnect Ping Latency Statistics

  • Ping latency of the roundtrip of a message from this instance to
  • target instances.
  • The target instance is identified by an instance number.
  • Average and standard deviation of ping latency is given in microseconds
  • for message sizes of 500 bytes and 8K.
  • Note that latency of a message from the instance to itself is used as
  • control, since message latency can include wait for CPU
Target Instance500B Ping CountAvg Latency 500B msgStddev 500B msg8K Ping CountAvg Latency 8K msgStddev 8K msg
